title: Is it because the sysctl instruction cannot be used in rootless mode, or because podman and docker have similar bugs?
body:
```
podman run --sysctl net.core.rmem_max=250000 alpine
Error: open /proc/sys/net/core/rmem_max: No such file or directory: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found
```
https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/30778

comments.body:
This looks like /proc/sys/net/core/rmem_max is not a namespaced field. so it does not show up within container.
```
# podman run fedora ls  /proc/sys/net/core/
somaxconn
xfrm_acq_expires
xfrm_aevent_etime
xfrm_aevent_rseqth
xfrm_larval_drop
# ls  /proc/sys/net/core/
bpf_jit_enable	  busy_poll		    dev_weight			  flow_limit_cpu_bitmap     max_skb_frags  netdev_budget_usecs	   netdev_unregister_timeout_secs  rps_sock_flow_entries  wmem_default	     xfrm_aevent_rseqth
bpf_jit_harden	  busy_read		    dev_weight_rx_bias		  flow_limit_table_len	    message_burst  netdev_max_backlog	   optmem_max			   somaxconn		  wmem_max	     xfrm_larval_drop
bpf_jit_kallsyms  default_qdisc		    dev_weight_tx_bias		  gro_normal_batch	    message_cost   netdev_rss_key	   rmem_default			   tstamp_allow_data	  xfrm_acq_expires
bpf_jit_limit	  devconf_inherit_init_net  fb_tunnels_only_for_init_net  high_order_alloc_disable  netdev_budget  netdev_tstamp_prequeue  rmem_max			   warnings		  xfrm_aevent_etime
```
You would need to take this up with the Linux kernel.
BTW you do do --net=host, but then the sysctl will effect the entire system.

Can I only need to execute the `sudo sysctl -w net.core.rmem_max=2500000` command in the host?
According to my understanding, changes in the host will affect the container.
